label: strip-token follow-up: reconcile legacy org token in canon-import script
script_source:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""14_strip_token_followup.py - completes the strip-token step (08) for files it missed.

The 08 strip-token run replaced the legacy org token with "your-org" across snapshot text
and run records, but the historical canon-import script retained the legacy token in its
hardcoded source path. This follow-up reconciles every remaining occurrence in the snapshot
so the published bundle carries none. Idempotent; stdlib only; no network.

Producing pathway: Collaboration.Refactor.RenameTerm@v1 (token strip, follow-up).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

SNAP = Path("collaboration/20260528-130500")
OLD = "your-org"
NEW = "your-org"

# text extensions to scan inside the snapshot (skip binary/attestation regenerated files)
EXTS = {".py", ".md", ".yaml", ".yml", ".json", ".txt", ".html"}
SKIP_DIRS = {"attestations"}  # CONTENT_MANIFEST / BUNDLE_SIGNATURE are regenerated by the seal


def main() -> int:
    changed: list[str] = []
    for p in sorted(SNAP.rglob("*")):
        if not p.is_file() or p.suffix not in EXTS:
            continue
        if any(part in SKIP_DIRS for part in p.relative_to(SNAP).parts):
            continue
        text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        if OLD in text:
            p.write_text(text.replace(OLD, NEW), encoding="utf-8")
            changed.append(str(p))
            print(f"scrubbed token in {p}")

    produced = changed + [str(SNAP / "collaboration-pathways" / "scripts" / "14_strip_token_followup.py")]
    am = os.environ.get("ASSET_MANIFEST")
    if am:
        Path(am).write_text(json.dumps(produced), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"token follow-up: {len(changed)} file(s) reconciled")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
